creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: 6 Things to Know about AIE World's Fair 2026
videos.summary: AI Engineer announces AIE World's Fair 2026 in San Francisco, highlighting expo floor, research-industry synergy, leadership tracks, and focus on AI verticals. The channel offers YouTube-exclusive discount codes and $40k in sponsor offers for attendees.

videos.title: The Production AI Playbook: Deploying Agents at Enterprise Scale — Sandipan Bhaumik, Databricks
videos.summary: Sandipan Bhaumik presents five pillars for deploying AI agents at enterprise scale: evaluation (define success before coding), observability (trace every decision for regulatory compliance), data foundation (agents require high-quality data), multi-agent orchestration, and governance. The talk draws from a retail bank case where six weeks of evaluation, tracing, and measurement infrastructure preceded model selection, and demonstrates how observability caught policy-induced stale embeddings post-launch.

videos.title: Your Agent's Biggest Lie: "I Searched the Web" — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates that agents often falsely report successful web searches when they are actually blocked, hit CAPTCHAs, or fall back to stale data. The presentation compares blocked versus unblocked agent behavior across sites like LinkedIn, Instagram, Amazon, and TikTok, showing how proper web access reduces hallucinations and improves citation reliability.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA achieved real-time video diffusion on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce denoising steps from 50 to 1-8. The approach stacks additive optimization techniques that each contribute independently to 10-200x speedup gains.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: AI engineers use agent loops that automatically prompt themselves rather than manual prompting. An orchestrator agent spawns worker agents in parallel to execute tasks, with observability dashboards tracking all runs. This approach scales agent behavior but requires careful cost management and hallucination mitigation.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Pi) as interchangeable workers under a unified interface. It enables task routing, multi-model workflows, code review automation, human-in-the-loop governance, and cross-device session persistence for AI-driven development tasks.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 model was withdrawn from availability following a US government export-control directive related to a claimed jailbreak vulnerability. Cole Medin discusses the circumstances of the pulldown and shares benchmark results comparing Fable 5 to Claude Opus 4.8.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that simplifies building and deploying production-grade AI agents. It provides features including agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails, enabling developers to ship agents to production in minutes.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason covers Loop Engineer, a tool for building AI agents, providing setup guidance and a GitHub repository with code templates and knowledge resources for implementation.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video explaining how to construct proactive AI agents capable of self-improvement using autonomous loops. Covers use cases including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with reference to tools like Loopany that enable memory and tool integration for these systems.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses /goal feature and autonomous coding workflows, comparing them to Ralph-loop 2.0 and suggesting real autonomous coders are emerging. The video covers /goal setup, prompting goals, goal-buddy functionality, and /Mission exploration for long horizontal tasks.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding paradigm discussed by AI Jason. The video covers how it works, harness setup with auto-testing capabilities, and workflow configuration through a WORKFLOW.md file.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: GPT explained visually..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code provides a visual walkthrough of generative pretrained transformer architecture and mechanics. The video covers self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections as core building blocks of GPT-based models including Gemini, Opus, Kimi, Nemotron, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's Bigram Language Model, covering tokenization, embeddings, and training fundamentals. The video serves as a foundation for understanding larger models like GPT, incorporating key concepts such as cross-entropy loss, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three variants: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture, Mixture of Experts via Latent MoE, and MTP (likely Mixture of Token Pools or similar) to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a language model that implements Sparse Attention (MSA) instead of full attention, with optimized KV cache reads and tiled I/O improvements. The architecture reduces bottlenecks in both prefill and decode stages of inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: Tool calling is a technique that enables AI agents to request external tools and services rather than relying solely on language model parameters. The video explains tool schema, system prompts that govern tool selection, and the agent-tool loop where models request tools and frameworks execute them.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with tools, up-to-date documentation, best practices, and guardrails to work effectively with AWS services. It improves agent success rates by providing tested procedures instead of relying on potentially outdated general knowledge.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock while maintaining data residency within AWS. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers video addresses strategies for tech job hunting in 2026 amid AI screening. Covers personal branding, networking advantages over mass applications, and argues junior developers remain valuable as jobs evolve rather than disappear.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech video comparing how engineers at OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google approach code generation. The video uses "vibing" as slang for the coding practice and includes future-looking segments on Google's approach in 2025 and 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or tool that the video frames as having a transformative or harmful impact on open source software development. The video explores what Malus does, comparisons to prior controversies, and implications for open source maintainers.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that code is only one component of a software moat; customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity are the real advantages. He contends that fears of AI eliminating SaaS companies are overblown and that the best AI products create devoted customers rather than merely satisfied ones.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan, a Stanford roboticist, argues that the coming wave of humanoid robots from companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision. She contends that designing robots for human delight and interaction, rather than utility alone, will be critical as billions of robots enter everyday life.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Pigment is an AI performance management platform that consolidates finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data into a single interface. Co-founded by Eléonore Crespo, it has raised over 400 million dollars and counts Figma, Brex, and Anthropic as customers.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that real product-market fit manifests as customer pull (inquiries about pricing) rather than push (enthusiasm from passionate users), and that founders should embed in customer workflows rather than rely on formal interviews. He emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories rather than current state.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: The piece argues that small agile teams are more productive and efficient than large teams, which become bogged down in excessive meetings. It advocates for smaller team units as a superior organizational structure.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: AI demand is driving growth in high bandwidth memory, with Micron and SK Hynix positioned as key vendors benefiting from the expansion. The video forecasts significant future growth in the memory stack market for these companies.

videos.title: Why Aren't Businesses Using AI Agents Yet? Experts Explain! #shorts
videos.summary: Agentic AI adoption is slow because the technology is still in its infancy, not due to lack of potential. This mirrors early-stage LLM adoption, with businesses now racing to harness the emerging capability.

videos.title: Intel's CPU Stagnation Ended By AMD's Fierce Competition! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof argues that AMD's competition broke Intel's dominance and spurred CPU innovation, resulting in more powerful and affordable processors. The video frames competitive pressure as beneficial for the broader market and consumers.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
